jokes.text: Well, needless to say, people are still talking about me in Barcelona. / I bet they are.
jokes.setup: Murray boasts about his fame in Barcelona
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's sarcastic 'I bet they are' deflates Murray's delusion
jokes.explanation: Classic deadpan undercut - Jemaine's delivery style perfectly deflates Murray's grandiosity

jokes.text: Murray, who's this guy? / I'm getting to that, Jemaine, all right? That's in the agenda under Todd.
jokes.setup: Natural question about unexpected person in room
jokes.punchline: Murray's rigid adherence to agenda despite obvious confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Murray's bureaucratic obsessiveness creates comedy from mundane situation

jokes.text: Now I wasn't sure whether that was the magazine or the band. I've heard from both, I've got a no from the band...
jokes.setup: Confusion between Rolling Stone magazine and Rolling Stones band
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions rejection from the actual Rolling Stones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - the casual mention of Rolling Stones rejection as throwaway detail is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Do you want me to go back to item one? / Yeah, go back to item one. / Item one: Todd. Yeah, he's the new band member. / Hi. / Right. Item two...
jokes.setup: Request to return to agenda item one
jokes.punchline: Murray immediately tries to move on after minimal explanation
jokes.explanation: Murray's robotic meeting management creates absurdist comedy from bureaucratic incompetence

jokes.text: Whoa!
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's surprised reaction to presumably mediocre bongo performance
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - Jemaine's deadpan surprise at ordinary bongo playing

jokes.text: Even better than yesterday. You could see all the details when your fingers bounce off the skins. Pigskin, is it?
jokes.punchline: Overly technical analysis of basic drumming, asking about pigskin
jokes.explanation: Murray's pseudo-expertise creates comedy through pretentious analysis of simple performance

jokes.text: I'll just tell you straight up that all I'm really interested in is kicking some grooves, banging some tunes, I don't know, just basically getting it on.
jokes.punchline: Cliched musician speak ending with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Todd's tryhard musician persona creates cringe comedy through overused terminology

jokes.text: Come on, Todd, we're having a private band meeting. / No, I mean without Todd.
jokes.punchline: Murray misunderstands and includes Todd in 'private' meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - Murray's obliviousness creates immediate comedic reversal

jokes.text: Sometimes I have good ideas. / When? / Like that time I saw you for the first time onstage. I said 'Face the front,' and you did.
jokes.setup: Murray claims he has good ideas, challenged by Jemaine
jokes.punchline: Murray's 'good idea' is basic stage direction
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Murray's pathetic example of management genius is perfectly delusional

jokes.text: I'd just been given nitrous oxide. I was at the dentist. I didn't know what I was talking about. I think I'm going to his brother's wedding.
jokes.punchline: Escalates from nitrous oxide excuse to wedding commitment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Murray's excuses spiral into increasingly personal commitments

jokes.text: You're like a proper band. You're like The Policemen.
jokes.setup: Murray comparing them to famous bands
jokes.punchline: Gets The Police wrong as 'The Policemen'
jokes.explanation: Classic Murray malapropism - slightly wrong band name reveals his musical ignorance

jokes.text: Murray, I'm on a... call here with the Secretary of State. Could you... how much longer are you gonna be?
jokes.setup: Band meeting happening in Murray's office
jokes.punchline: Important diplomatic work interrupted by band drama
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy - juxtaposition of diplomatic importance vs band trivia

jokes.text: Give Peace a Chance. / Give Pete a Chance. / Peace.
jokes.punchline: Gets it wrong as 'Pete', Jemaine corrects deadpan
jokes.explanation: Another Murray malapropism with Jemaine's patient correction highlighting the absurdity

jokes.text: Can we try a song that I wrote real quick? / No.
jokes.setup: Todd asks to try his song
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's immediate flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan rejection - Jemaine's bluntness cuts through Todd's enthusiasm

jokes.text: All you guys'll do, you'll put your guitars and bass down and you just go, 'Arf arf!' In the background, like dogs. Like bad dogs.
jokes.punchline: Wants band members to bark like dogs
jokes.explanation: Absurd creative vision that foreshadows 'Doggy Bounce' - Todd's terrible musical ideas

jokes.text: I dance when I'm angry. / When you're angry? Who dances when they're angry?
jokes.setup: Bret claims he dances when angry
jokes.punchline: Todd's logical confusion about angry dancing
jokes.explanation: Good character quirk - Bret's unusual emotional expression creates believable weirdness

jokes.text: Whoa-ho! What are the chances? We're always bumping into each other. / About one in one.
jokes.setup: Mel acts surprised at running into them
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's literal mathematical response about certainty
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan logic - Jemaine's mathematical literalism deflates Mel's fake surprise perfectly

jokes.text: Actually, he, um, neglected to tell me what a hottie you are. / Stop it. / Looks like the race is on. Gentlemen, start your engines. Vroom! Vroom!
jokes.punchline: Awkward car racing metaphor for romantic competition
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from Todd's terrible pickup lines and metaphors

jokes.text: What do you play, Todd? / I'll give you a clue. / The bongos. / Oh! Rhythmic. / Yeah, tribal. / Powerful. Very physical, if you know what I mean.
jokes.punchline: Todd's sexual innuendo about bongo playing being 'physical'
jokes.explanation: Todd's attempt to make bongo playing sound sexy creates predictable cringe

jokes.text: She's a maniac. / In the sack? Really? / No. What?
jokes.punchline: Todd immediately assumes sexual context, Jemaine's confused reaction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Todd's mind in gutter contrasts with Jemaine's innocent meaning

jokes.text: Oh, I thought you had a threesome with her. / No, she's just a fan. Never had a threesome with her.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's matter-of-fact denial with unnecessary specificity
jokes.explanation: Jemaine's deadpan clarification of never having threesome with fan is perfectly awkward

jokes.text: Who cares? I'm a bongo player in a band. She's hot to trot. I'm gonna make it happen. I'm gonna give it to her.
jokes.setup: Todd explaining why Mel's marriage doesn't matter
jokes.punchline: String of crude clichés and overconfidence about his 'musician' status
jokes.explanation: Establishes Todd as stereotypical sleazy musician. Clichéd language fits his character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, if you two homo sapiens aren't gonna make the move, guess what? I'm gonna.
jokes.punchline: Uses 'homo sapiens' instead of understanding it means human
jokes.explanation: Todd's misuse of scientific term reveals his pseudo-intellectual posturing

jokes.text: He's the Pied Piper of cool. / Pied Piper was a good musician, wasn't he? / He was cool. / He wasn't cool. / Yes, he was. / He took all those kids into a cave.
jokes.setup: Murray comparing Todd to Pied Piper
jokes.punchline: Bret points out the dark child-stealing aspect
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of fairy tale reference - Bret's dark logic undermines Murray's metaphor

jokes.text: No, before that phase, when just the rats followed him...
jokes.setup: Murray defending his Pied Piper comparison
jokes.punchline: Tries to salvage metaphor by focusing on the rat-catching phase
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the metaphor. Murray's attempt to make it work by focusing on rats is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Your solos were too long? / No no no, the band name is too long. / What is it? Flight of the Con... Con... Conchords... / Blah blah blah. Too long, too boring.
jokes.punchline: Todd criticizes the show's own name as too boring
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the show's own name being criticized by annoying character

jokes.text: Get ready... The Crazy Dogggz. Three Gs and a Z.
jokes.punchline: Terrible name with needlessly specified spelling
jokes.explanation: Todd's terrible band name with pretentious spelling explanation captures his tryhard nature

jokes.text: You're too easily offended. / I can't believe you just said that.
jokes.setup: Jemaine says Bret is easily offended
jokes.punchline: Bret immediately proves the point by being offended
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate irony - Bret proves Jemaine's point instantly through his reaction

jokes.text: I told him he was fired, he pushed me. / Then he said that you should be fired because you're the one holding us back. / Well, I hit him.
jokes.punchline: Escalates from push to hit over band loyalty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of violence over band drama, shows Jemaine's loyalty to Bret

jokes.text: Well, you see, while I had him in a headlock, he sort of explained why he thought you shouldn't be in the band. And after a while it kind of made sense.
jokes.punchline: Was convinced by Todd's argument while physically fighting him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - being convinced by argument during physical altercation

jokes.text: You were only over there for like 20 seconds. / Yeah, after about 20 seconds it kind of made sense.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine confirms he was swayed very quickly
jokes.explanation: Good callback to headlock joke - Jemaine's quick persuasion timeline is absurd

jokes.text: It's easy for me to fire you, Bret, because you're my friend.
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic that friendship makes firing easier
jokes.explanation: Perfect backwards logic - Jemaine's twisted friendship reasoning for betrayal

jokes.text: Flup!
jokes.punchline: Non-word expression of defeat/confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-verbal reaction. The made-up word captures Jemaine's complete bewilderment efficiently.

jokes.text: More enthusiasm this time, all right? / Todd, how about you get down on all fours and wag your tail like you just can't fail and I'll just play bass, huh?
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's sarcastic suggestion for Todd to act like a dog
jokes.explanation: Jemaine's passive-aggressive rebellion against Todd's terrible song direction

jokes.text: I'm not Gloria Estefan, all right?
jokes.setup: Bret being told not to do percussion
jokes.punchline: Random reference to Gloria Estefan to reject rhythm section role
jokes.explanation: Bret's oddly specific pop culture reference to resist percussion duties

jokes.text: It's not about the music. You know what it's about? # The pussy! #
jokes.punchline: Crude sung declaration about sexual motivation
jokes.explanation: Todd's crude honesty about musical motivation creates cringe through vulgarity

jokes.text: Do you guys need to borrow my amp? / Bret, you're pathetic, man. We don't need a roadie, okay?
jokes.setup: Bret offering to help with equipment
jokes.punchline: Todd dismissing him as a roadie instead of band member
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's desperation and Todd's cruelty. The roadie dismissal is appropriately harsh.

jokes.text: So is a guitar or a keyboard? / Exactly. It's the best of both worlds.
jokes.punchline: Demetri's non-answer that it's 'exactly' both
jokes.explanation: Demetri's nonsensical answer about keytar being both instruments simultaneously

jokes.text: No, don't like it. Ugh, no. Weird. / Oh, that bit's good.
jokes.punchline: Immediate negative reaction followed by brief positive note
jokes.explanation: Murray's inconsistent reaction to keytar performance shows his unreliable judgment

jokes.text: Oh, here we go. The Fonzies have arrived.
jokes.punchline: Demetri calls them 'Fonzies' as insult
jokes.explanation: Demetri's dated reference to Happy Days character as insult about coolness

jokes.text: It's called the Original Flight of the Conchords. You guys can be called Flight of the Condors, or whatever.
jokes.punchline: Bret suggests similar but wrong bird name
jokes.explanation: Bret's passive-aggressive band name suggestion using wrong bird species

jokes.text: We shouldn't even be arguing in front of the map. It's not right.
jokes.punchline: Murray's misplaced propriety about arguing near New Zealand map
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Murray's respect for the map over actual diplomatic work is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: What do you play, the leather jacket?
jokes.punchline: Jemaine asks if he plays his clothing as instrument
jokes.explanation: Classic Jemaine deadpan observation about Demetri's rock star costume

jokes.text: We shouldn't even be arguing in front of the map.
jokes.setup: The band arguing in Murray's office
jokes.punchline: Murray's reverence for the office map as if it has feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray moment showing his misplaced respect for government property over human relationships.

jokes.text: Well, I was trying to fire Todd but he did a sad face at me.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine defeated by basic facial expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jemaine's conflict avoidance expressed through childlike reasoning

jokes.text: I'm not even suppose to manage one band. I'm suppose to be working at the New Zealand Consulate, Bret.
jokes.setup: Murray stressed about managing two bands
jokes.punchline: Admits he's neglecting actual diplomatic job
jokes.explanation: Murray's admission of job neglect adds stakes to his band management obsession

jokes.text: Does he play any other instruments? No. Come on, what? Is that what you want? I'm gonna break your keytar. I'm gonna break your keytar. You think I care?
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous threat to break the keytar and Demetri's indifference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd conflict and Demetri's casual attitude toward his instrument being threatened.

jokes.text: # The distant future, the year 2000 # / # Arf arf arf arf #
jokes.setup: Two bands playing different songs simultaneously
jokes.punchline: Serious sci-fi song vs dog barking song played together
jokes.explanation: Brilliant musical juxtaposition - sophisticated vs ridiculous songs create comedic chaos

jokes.text: It's going pretty good, except that he copies my moves. / What moves? / My dance moves. / You don't have dance moves.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine points out Bret has no dance moves to copy
jokes.explanation: Jemaine deflating Bret's ego with logical observation about nonexistent dance skills

jokes.text: Todd and I are going to start a group... our own group without Bret and Jerome.
jokes.punchline: Gets Jemaine's name wrong as 'Jerome'
jokes.explanation: Simple name mistake adds insult to injury of band betrayal

jokes.text: I'm flabbergasted.
jokes.punchline: Murray's formal, understated reaction to shocking news
jokes.explanation: Murray's formal vocabulary for emotional shock fits his bureaucratic character

jokes.text: Hey, you got one of the Crazy Dogggz t-shirts. / No. / It says Crazy Dogggz on it.
jokes.punchline: Mel's denial despite obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment of Mel caught supporting Todd's band despite denial

jokes.text: Hey, Mel, you didn't make it to our gig the other... / Oh, yeah. / There was no one there... without you there.
jokes.setup: Jemaine mentioning Mel missed their show
jokes.punchline: Reveals their audience was just Mel - without her, literally no one attended
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of devastating truth. Shows their complete lack of audience in the most understated way possible.

jokes.text: Number one in 24 countries. / They say hi? / No.
jokes.setup: Murray bragging about Crazy Dogggz success
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's hopeful question met with blunt 'no'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation - Jemaine's hope for reconciliation crushed by Murray's honesty

jokes.text: How giant is it? / That's what it's called... Giant. So I'm guessing pretty big.
jokes.punchline: Murray's literal interpretation of stadium name
jokes.explanation: Murray's literal-mindedness about stadium naming conventions

jokes.text: Giant Stadium. / How giant is it? / That's what it's called... Giant. So I'm guessing pretty big.
jokes.punchline: Murray's literal interpretation of the name as size indicator
jokes.explanation: Good literalism gag. Murray's logical but naive interpretation of stadium naming conventions.

jokes.text: I've got lunch with Tori Amos.
jokes.setup: Murray explaining why he has to leave
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of lunch with famous musician
jokes.explanation: Simple name-drop absurdity. Murray's casual mention of celebrity lunch fits his delusional networking claims.
